about.description: I'm Adriel, product designer and trainer. My mission: simplify creating SaaS, inspire and guide those who want to bring their ideas to life.
about.keywords: about Adriel Zimbril, SaaS Product Designer, SaaS trainer, product design journey SaaS, ShiroSaaS method

craft-section.description: Put the user at the center of every decision, guide and teach creators to transform every idea into unforgettable experiences.
craft-section.title: Turning ideas into products that matter.

header-section.description: Since my beginnings in design, I've learned that the most successful product isn't the one you see first, but the one you understand, test and evolve with intention.
header-section.description-secondary:
Sic itur ad astra — that's how we reach the stars 🫰🏻.

Since I was a child, I've always been fascinated by technology and invention. Iron Man gave me a taste for the possible: I remember my eyes sparkling when I saw his suit and the inspiration that crossed me with every episode. I spent hours imagining, tinkering, and dreaming of creating my own robots.

By giving free rein to this passion I discovered design and over time I understood that my dream wasn't limited to building machines: I wanted to create experiences that touch people, solve real problems and make people want to use them. Design became my playground, my laboratory to imagine, build and transform raw ideas into concrete products and design experiences that matter.

Today, I turn ideas into tangible experiences, supporting founders, teams and creators to clarify their problems, imagine solutions and launch them quickly. My approach combines thoughtful design, agile strategy and continuous learning so each project has real impact.

I like to question what exists, explore what works and what doesn't, and build intentionally. I prefer to do things with a why rather than by habit.

At the same time, I share methods and resources from my creative experiences so others can learn to design and launch products themselves, with confidence.

For me, design is not an isolated deliverable: it's a collaborative, creative and human process.
header-section.title:
Hello, I'm 👋 
 Adriel Zimbril 🐼 
 Product Designer & Trainer
header-section.title-secondary:
Create, simplify, inspire 🧑🏻‍🚀 
 Welcome to my world 🌌

content.conclusion: Facts, numbers, painful failures and unexpected victories. Design as it really happens ❤️.
content.item1: What works (and what doesn't) in the interfaces I create
content.item2: User feedback that made me change my mind
content.item3: My mistakes and how I try to avoid them
content.subtitle: My observations on :
content.title: In this newsletter, I simply share what I learn.

cta.description: A guide on UX mistakes I often notice in SaaS. Concrete observations I've gathered from my projects.
cta.note: And of course, you can unsubscribe whenever you want. I write to you only when I have something useful to share.
cta.title: I prepared something to get started.

newsletterSignup.preview: Hi ! I wanted to talk to you about something close to my heart...

story.intro: A few weeks ago, one of the clients of one of my apps wrote to tell me that they struggled to cancel an action. I realized it was because of a design choice I liked… but which complicated the experience.
story.philosophy: What really interests me is understanding how people use what we create. Why they abandon at a certain point, why they come back, and what really helps them in their daily lives.
story.presentation: My name is Adriel, and for 5 years I've been creating interfaces for SaaS and I've noticed something: we often complicate things while users are just looking for simplicity.

newsletterSignup.subject: Hello, it's Adriel !
newsletterSignup.tagline: Problem Solver & Your SaaS Product Designer 🦄

why.motivation: I want to share these small discoveries, those moments when you say "oh, I wouldn't have thought of that". Without pretension, just shared curiosity ❤️.
why.reason: Because I like to understand how things work. And in design, there is often a gap between what we think will work and what actually works.
why.title: Why I really write ?

productDelivery.message1: I'm glad you're part of this adventure. I hope this resource helps you go further, faster.
productDelivery.message2: Your feedback really helps me improve my content for future people. And if you have questions along the way, don't hesitate to ask!

code.feedback: When you've had time to browse it, I'd like to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? What still puzzles you?
code.tip: 🚀 Tip: try to apply what you learn directly.

course.feedback: After watching some modules, I'd love your feedback. Does the content meet your expectations? Are there points you'd like to dive deeper into?
course.tip: 💡 Tip: take notes while going through the modules, it really helps to retain and apply.

ebook.feedback: When you've had time to browse it, I'd like to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? What still puzzles you?
ebook.tip: 📖 Suggestion: read it in small sections and try to apply what you learn directly.

figma_template.feedback: When you've had time to check it, I'd like to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? What still puzzles you?
figma_template.tip: 🚀 Tip: try to apply what you learn directly.

masterclass.feedback: Once you've watched the masterclass, tell me what you think! Did it answer your questions? What stood out the most?
masterclass.tip: 🎯 Tip: watch it in a quiet place with a notebook nearby. The best ideas often come while listening.

video.feedback: Once you've watched the video, tell me what you think! Did it answer your questions? What stood out the most?
video.tip: 🎥 Tip: watch it in a quiet place with a notebook nearby. The best ideas often come while listening.

string.inclusive: String must contain at least {minimum} character(s)
